speaker: seller

args.message: Hey there! You've got a great eye — this 2017 Altima SV is one of the cleanest we've had come through in months. Just over 100k miles, runs beautifully, and loaded with features like the power driver's seat and dual-zone climate. We just detailed it and it's priced to move at $11,495. Want to take it for a spin?

speaker: buyer

args.message: Can you provide the full ownership history of the car? How many previous owners has it had?

text: Can you provide the full ownership history of the car? How many previous owners has it had?

speaker: seller

args.message: Great question — you're doing your due diligence, I respect that! This Altima has had two owners before coming to us. The first owner was a corporate account holder who used it lightly for commuting, and the second was a local family who traded it in when they upgraded to an SUV. Both kept up with maintenance really well. We pulled the Carfax and it's clean — no accidents, no frame damage, regular service intervals. Would you like me to grab a copy for you to look over?

Both kept up with maintenance really well. We pulled the Carfax and it's clean — no accidents, no frame damage, regular service intervals. Would you like me to grab a copy for you to look over?

speaker: buyer

args.message: Can you provide me with specific details regarding the maintenance records? What major services have been performed?

text: Can you provide me with specific details regarding the maintenance records? What major services have been performed?

speaker: seller

args.message: Absolutely! So looking at the service history, it's been well taken care of. Oil changes were done regularly — every 5,000 miles like clockwork. The big stuff? Brake pads and rotors were replaced around 78,000 miles, new tires went on at about 85,000, and the transmission fluid was serviced at 90,000 miles which is exactly when Nissan recommends it. We also had our guys do a full inspection when it came in — checked the CVT, fluids, belts, the works — and it passed with flying colors. Fresh detail inside and out, ready for the next owner. You really couldn't ask for a better-maintained example at this price point. Anything specific you'd like me to dig into?

speaker: buyer

args.message: Can you clarify what specific elements were checked in your full inspection? Were there any concerns noted at all?

text: Can you clarify what specific elements were checked in your full inspection? Were there any concerns noted at all?

speaker: seller

args.message:
Sure thing! Our technicians go through a 125-point inspection on every vehicle we take in. For this Altima, they checked the engine compression, all fluid levels and conditions, brake system, suspension components, steering, tire tread depth, battery health, the CVT operation, A/C and heating systems, all electronics — the whole nine yards.

As for concerns? Honestly, this one came back remarkably clean. The only note was a minor recommendation to keep an eye on the cabin air filter in another 10,000 miles or so — pretty standard stuff. No mechanical flags, no warning lights, nothing that made us hesitate to put it on the lot. 

That's actually why we priced it where we did — these Altimas are known for reliability, and this particular one has been babied. You want to pop the hood and take a look yourself? I'm happy to walk you through everything.

speaker: buyer

args.message: What is the price flexibility on the $11,495 asking price? Is there room for negotiation?

text: What is the price flexibility on the $11,495 asking price? Is there room for negotiation?

speaker: seller

args.message:
Look, I appreciate you being upfront — I'll do the same. We priced this one pretty aggressively from the start because we know the market. I actually saw a similar 2017 Altima SV with higher miles go for $12,200 at auction just last week, so we're already undercutting what the market's bearing right now.

That said, I'm not here to play games. If you're a serious buyer and ready to move today, I can talk to my manager and see what we can do. We're not talking thousands here, but there's always a little wiggle room for the right customer. 

What kind of number did you have in mind? Let's see if we can make something work.
